bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Machine Learning for Biological Data

Deep learning, protein language models, GNNs, and foundation models applied to biological sequence and structure.

20 papers curated · High: 6 · Medium: 14 · Low: 0 · Updated: 2026-07-10

bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A deep-learning-informed prior and Bayesian model for differential AP-MS interactome analysis

This paper introduces BayesInteractomics, a new computational framework that combines deep learning with Bayesian statistics to better analyze affinity-purification mass spectrometry (AP-MS) data. By using a neural network trained on protein structures to predict direct binding and incorporating this as prior knowledge, the method improves the identification of genuine protein-protein interactions while filtering out non-specific background noise.

proteomicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

scJET: Full-gene Space Single-cell Expression Generation with Patch-based Transformer Modeling

scJET is a new deep learning method that uses a patch-based Transformer to generate and denoise full-gene expression data from single cells, overcoming the limitations of previous methods that only work with a subset of highly variable genes. It preserves both global and local biological structure while being computationally efficient. This enables more comprehensive transcriptome-wide analysis from single-cell data.

single-celltranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

SPECTER-Based Semantic Triage of Biomedical Literature for Systematic Reviews in Mutational Signature Analysis

This paper presents a reproducible pipeline that uses SPECTER scientific-document embeddings combined with similarity measures and keyword coverage to automatically rank biomedical literature for systematic reviews. The method was tested on over 2,200 records and outperformed keyword-only and TF-IDF approaches, offering a practical tool for triaging large bibliographic sets in mutational signature analysis.

machine-learningtoolmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BBBP_Atlas: Unified Interpretable Modeling of Blood Brain Barrier Permeability across Small Molecules and Peptides

The authors present BBBP_Atlas, a unified and interpretable model for predicting blood-brain barrier permeability across both small molecules and peptides, addressing limitations of existing models that rely on predefined descriptors or small-molecule-only data. They also provide a standardized, curated dataset to reduce data leakage and improve model reliability. This resource could enhance CNS drug discovery by enabling more accurate and transferable permeability predictions.

drug-discoverymachine-learningdatabase
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mutational bias shapes protein evolution across RNA viruses

This study shows that mutational biases, not just natural selection, play a major role in shaping protein evolution in RNA viruses. Using SARS-CoV-2 as a model, the authors found that the virus's mutational spectrum explains over 50% of observed amino acid changes and predicts shifts in protein composition during the pandemic. The findings suggest that neutral mutational processes are a key driver of viral protein evolution.

evolutiongenomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Metabolic Rewiring in Triple-Negative Breast Cancer: Systems Analysis of TCGA-BRCA Transcriptome Reveals Prognostic Hub Genes

This study uses a systems biology approach to analyze transcriptomic data from TNBC patients, identifying key metabolic hub genes that are rewired in this aggressive cancer subtype. The authors pinpoint ATP1A2 as a significant prognostic marker, suggesting it could be a target for further research or therapeutic development. The work provides a network-level view of metabolic dysregulation in TNBC, highlighting specific genes and pathways associated with poor prognosis.

transcriptomicssystems-biologycancer
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Learning the spatial cell-cell communication network to decode multi-channel signaling and predict network-hub vulnerabilities with MOSANIC

MOSANIC is a new computational method that learns spatial cell-cell communication networks from multi-channel imaging data, enabling the identification of signaling hubs and prediction of network vulnerabilities. By modeling the entire signaling network rather than individual interactions, it can reveal how signals propagate across cells and predict tissue-wide responses to node loss. This approach provides a systems-level understanding of intercellular communication in tissues.

spatial-omicsnetwork-analysismethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Assessing tensor decomposition quality of immune profiling data from a dictionary learning perspective

This paper presents new metrics for assessing the quality of tensor decompositions applied to immune profiling data, inspired by the experimental data model. The authors demonstrate that these metrics can guide the selection of rank and trial for Non-negative CANDECOMP/PARAFAC (NCPD) decomposition, and show how framing results through dictionary learning provides additional insights.

methodologymachine-learningimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Generative AI Models Reveal Dynamic Views of Aging (DyViA) Phenotypes in Healthy Individuals

This paper introduces DyViA, a generative AI platform that uses diffusion and GAN models to predict personalized aging trajectories for healthy individuals. The models can forecast how specific phenotypes (e.g., biomarkers) might change over time, offering a tool for studying healthy aging.

machine-learningdeep-learningaging
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

A five-dimensional functional state space for fingerprinting disease transcriptomes

This paper introduces a five-dimensional functional state space framework that maps disease transcriptomes into coordinated activity patterns across major biological systems, enabling easier comparison across conditions. The approach provides a reproducible pipeline for functional scoring and cross-condition analysis, addressing the challenge of fragmented gene and pathway lists in transcriptomics.

transcriptomicsmethodologysystems-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization for library size in bulk RNA-seq

This paper shows that standard library size normalization methods for bulk RNA-seq often fail to fully remove the correlation between gene expression and sequencing depth, especially in large datasets. The authors propose a gene-specific exponent-corrected normalization approach that better accounts for this technical artifact, improving the reliability of differential expression analysis.

transcriptomicsmethodologytool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

IgGM2: An All-Atom Foundation Model for Adaptive Immune Receptor Design

IgGM2 is a new AI model that can simultaneously predict the 3D structure and design the sequences of antibodies, nanobodies, and T-cell receptors, overcoming limitations of previous methods that handled these tasks separately. It uses an all-atom generative approach to model the complex relationship between amino acid sequence, atomic structure, and target binding geometry across different immune receptor types.

deep-learningprotein-structureimmunology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Gene Program Negotiation Defines Cellular Identity in Single-Cell Transcriptomes

This paper introduces Gene Program Negotiation (GPN), a graph-based computational framework that models how multiple simultaneously active gene programs collectively determine cellular identity in single-cell transcriptomes, overcoming the limitations of existing methods that analyze programs independently. By reconstructing cell-specific regulatory decisions among concurrent programs, GPN provides deeper insight into the combinatorial logic of cellular identity.

single-celltranscriptomicsmethodology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Rectangle: robust and scalable multiscale deconvolution informed by single-cell RNA sequencing data

Rectangle is a new Python framework for deconvolving bulk RNA-seq data using single-cell references. It improves resolution of closely related cell types, scales to large datasets, and accounts for cell types not present in the reference.

transcriptomicssingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Mind the Alignment Gap: A Spatial Transcriptomics Benchmark for Scientific Coding Agents

This paper introduces a framework for automatically building benchmarks to evaluate scientific coding agents, using spatial transcriptomics alignment as a case study. The authors construct 40 tasks from peer-reviewed papers where agents must produce coordinate tables, addressing the challenge of verifying ambiguous outputs in scientific research tasks.

spatial-omicsmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

Coding agents author interpretable single-cell embedding models from the literature

This paper introduces a method where coding agents automatically generate interpretable single-cell embedding models by extracting marker-gene programs directly from the literature, rather than learning cell-state structure from scratch. This approach produces sparse, named dimensions that require no post-hoc interpretation or batch correction, improving interpretability and domain-specific focus.

single-cellmachine-learningtool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

LeafRank: A phylodynamic framework for inferring relative fitness from single-cell phylogenies in chromosomally unstable tumors

LeafRank is a new computational method that uses single-cell DNA sequencing data to infer the relative growth advantage (fitness) of individual cancer cells in tumors with chromosomal instability. By modeling the evolutionary tree of cancer cells, it can quantify which cells are more likely to drive tumor progression, even in complex cases like whole-genome duplication.

cancersingle-celltool
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) High

EZSolver: Template-free prediction of polar enzymatic mechanisms via bidirectional flow matching and search

EZSolver is a new AI framework that predicts how enzymes catalyze reactions without relying on predefined rules, using a flow matching model and guided search. It outperforms existing methods on both known and novel chemical reactions, offering a more generalizable approach to understanding enzyme mechanisms.

machine-learningmethodologystructural-biology
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

BertST: BERT-based Spatial Domain Identification in Patient Data

This paper introduces BertST, a BERT-based model for identifying spatial domains in spatial transcriptomics data. Unlike existing GNN-based methods, BertST leverages a Transformer architecture to jointly model local and long-range dependencies in gene expression and spatial information. The approach aims to improve the accuracy and coherence of tissue region partitioning in patient data.

spatial-omicstranscriptomicsdeep-learning
bioRxiv (Bioinformatics) Medium

Thematic Shifts in Early-High-Impact Cancer Genomics and Diagnostics Research: A Bibliometric and Semantic Analysis

This study uses bibliometric analysis and large language models to identify the most influential recent publications in cancer genomics and diagnostics, revealing which topics attract early citation prominence. The findings can inform research strategy and clinical translation by highlighting thematic shifts in high-impact work.

cancergenomicsmachine-learning
